channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US Hockey team wants Trump at Canada final after brawl
item.description: Bill Guerin, general manager of the Minnesota Wild hockey team, invited President Trump to the Thursday 4-Nations Faceoff Championship game following the leader's history-making visit to New Orleans for the Super Bowl. “We would love it if President Trump was in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: The pope has pneumonia in both lungs but remains in good spirits, Vatican says
item.description: Francis was admitted to Rome’s Gemelli hospital in a “fair” condition on Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over 4 bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A top Hamas official says the militant group will free six living Israeli hostages on Saturday and return the bodies of four others on Thursday, a surprise acceleration in releases that apparently comes in return for Israel’s allowing mobile homes and construction equipment into the dev
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attacks by Sudanese RSF paramilitaries leave hundreds dead in White Nile State
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Attacks by Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Force have killed hundreds of civilians, including infants, in White Nile state, Sudanese officials and rights groups said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia frees detained American as talks with US on Ukraine begin
item.description: Russia has freed an American citizen detained on drug charges, in what the State Department called a "welcome gesture" ahead of a meeting between U.S. and Russian officials in Saudi Arabia. American Kalob Wayne Byers, 28, was released earlier this week to the custody of U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Delta plane crash: Passengers live after escaping ‘upside down’ jet
item.description: Air traffic control audio captured the moment officials realized Delta Flight 4819 was burning on the tarmac.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: OVER THE SCARBOROUGH SHOAL, South China Sea (AP) — A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet (3 meters) of a Philippine patrol plane on Tuesday in a disputed area of the South China Sea, prompting the Filipino pilot to warn by radio: “You are flying too close, you are very dangerous.
item.dc:creator: JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Russian, US officials meet in Saudi Arabia to begin talks on Ukraine war without officials from Kyiv
item.description: Senior Russian and American officials met in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday to begin talks on improving relations and negotiating an end to Moscow's war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border town's residents rebuild in south Lebanon as Hezbollah leader calls for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: KHIAM, Lebanon (AP) — Sabah Abdullah comes to her hometown in Lebanon every morning and sits next to her destroyed home. She is waiting for experts from Hezbollah's construction arm to compensate her for the damage caused by the Israel-Hezbollah war that has left her homeless.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and the US agree to work toward improving ties and ending the Ukraine war in landmark talks
item.description: Russia and the U.S. have agreed to start working toward ending the war in Ukraine and improving their diplomatic and economic ties.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Baraa Anwer And Dasha Litvinova, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine Vice President Duterte asks Supreme Court to void her impeachment and block Senate trial
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte asked the Supreme Court on Tuesday to void her impeachment and block a Senate trial that could remove her from office. The House of Representatives, which is dominated by the allies of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Now is an 'opportune' time to fix FAA: Former official Billy Nolen
item.description: "We certainly can never rest on our laurels when it comes to keeping the flying public safe," Nolen said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Delta plane crash: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he's 'in touch with Delta' after crash landing at Toronto airport
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) said Monday he was “in touch with Delta” following the crash landing of a flight at Toronto Pearson International Airport. The flight originated at Minneapolis-St. Paul International Airport (MSP) in Walz's state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump speaks with Macron ahead of European leaders meeting on Ukraine
item.description: President Trump spoke with French President Emmanuel Macron ahead of a European leaders' meeting about the war in Ukraine, as the Trump administration is pressing forward with plans to negotiate an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Delta jet crashes at Canada's Toronto Pearson Airport; crews responding to plane flipped upside down
item.description: Officials confirmed to Fox News Digital that Delta plane crashed at Toronto Pearson Airport on Monday afternoon. The CRJ-900 jet, operating as Delta 4819, departed from Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Many weary Ukrainians long for an end to the war but now fear it will come on unfavorable terms
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — After nearly three years of living under constant threat of Russian airstrikes while their troops fight a grinding campaign against Russia's invasion, many Ukrainians long for an end to the war — but now fear it could come on unfavorable terms. Top U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy travels to UAE as momentum grows for talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates on Monday as momentum grows for potential peace talks ending Russia's war on the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy not yet signing US economic agreement 'short-sighted,' White House official says
item.description: A White House national security official said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "is being short-sighted" in not signing a rare earth minerals deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here are some takeaways from the first month of Trump's Mideast diplomacy
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — When Hamas threatened to call off the planned release of three Israeli hostages last week, U.S. President Donald Trump stepped into the picture with an unexpected ultimatum.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China says US has 'gravely backpedaled' on Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's Foreign Ministry took issue Monday with a revised U.S. government fact sheet that removed a line on American opposition to independence for Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earth’s solid metal core may be soft on the outside: Study
item.description: Rather than being perfectly ball-shaped, the Earth's inner core may actually resemble peaks and valleys.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: At least 18 people dead after stampede at New Delhi railway station
item.description: “The crowd went out of control and no one could control it,” said Nikhil Kumar, a shopkeeper who witnessed the crowd surge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Emmanuel Macron calls 'emergency meeting' for European leaders to discuss Trump: report
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has allegedly planned an "emergency meeting" to discuss President Donald Trump on Monday, according to a Polish official.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American detained in Russia identified as Kalob Wayne Byers
item.description: Kalob Wayne Byers was identified as the American citizen recently detained at an airport in Moscow, Russia, on a drug smuggling charge, media reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Modi meet at Oval Office as US weighs tariffs
item.description: Before speaking with Trump, Modi met separately with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
